name: opencode-skills-notifier
description: OpenCode plugin that detects new skills in configured remote Git repositories and shows a toast notification on launch so team members never miss new shared tooling.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and shared types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TypeScript project scaffold (package.json, tsconfig.json) and shared type definitions so the plugin can be built and published to npm.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with name 'opencode-skills-notifier', type 'module', main 'dist/index.js'
package.json has @opencode-ai/plugin as peerDependency and devDependency
tsconfig.json targets ESNext with moduleResolution bundler and outputs to dist/
src/types.ts exports Cache, PluginConfig interfaces matching the plan data model
bun run build (tsc) completes without errors
bun run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Greenfield project root — create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/types.ts | Plugin type from @opencode-ai/plugin

userStories.title: Config parsing module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that reads the plugin's configuration section from ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json with safe defaults so the plugin works even if no config exists.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config.ts exports readPluginConfig(): Promise<PluginConfig>
Returns defaults { enabled: true, checkIntervalMinutes: 60, repositories: [], skillsScope: 'both' } when config file is missing
Returns defaults when opencode-skills-notifier section is absent from config
Correctly parses enabled, checkIntervalMinutes, repositories, skillsScope fields when present
Unit test: missing config file returns defaults
Unit test: partial config merges with defaults
Unit test: full config parses all fields correctly
/code-review passes, critical/important issues fixed
bun run typecheck passes
bun test passes

userStories.title: Cache read/write module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module to read and write the cache file that tracks last-checked timestamps, remote commit hashes per repo, and which skills have already been shown in a toast.
acceptanceCriteria: src/cache.ts exports readCache(): Promise<Cache> and writeCache(cache: Cache): Promise<void>
readCache returns { last_checked_at: '1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', repos: {}, notified_skills: [] } when file is missing
readCache returns { last_checked_at: '1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', repos: {}, notified_skills: [] } when file is malformed JSON
writeCache writes valid JSON to ~/.config/opencode/skills-notifier-cache.json
Round-trip: write then read returns identical Cache object
Unit test: missing cache file returns empty default
Unit test: malformed JSON returns empty default
Unit test: round-trip write then read
/code-review passes, critical/important issues fixed
bun run typecheck passes
bun test passes

userStories.title: Local skills detection and implicit repo discovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need functions to detect which skills are already installed locally and to discover skill repo URLs from the workspace's .agents/ directory so repos don't need to be configured manually.
acceptanceCriteria: src/discovery.ts exports getLocalSkills(directory, scope): Promise<Set<string>>
getLocalSkills with scope='global' scans ~/.agents/skills/ and returns folder names
getLocalSkills with scope='project' scans <directory>/.agents/skills/ and returns folder names
getLocalSkills with scope='both' returns union of global and project skills
getLocalSkills returns empty Set when directories don't exist (no throw)
src/discovery.ts exports discoverLocalRepos(directory): Promise<string[]>
discoverLocalRepos reads .git/config from each subdir of <directory>/.agents/skills/ and extracts [remote "origin"] url
discoverLocalRepos returns [] when .agents/skills/ doesn't exist
discoverLocalRepos deduplicates URLs
Unit test: getLocalSkills returns correct skill names from mocked directory
Unit test: getLocalSkills returns empty set when dir missing
Unit test: discoverLocalRepos extracts git remote URLs correctly
Unit test: discoverLocalRepos deduplicates identical URLs
/code-review passes, critical/important issues fixed
bun run typecheck passes
bun test passes
userStories.notes: src/discovery.ts | skills dirs: ~/.agents/skills/ (global), .agents/skills/ (project) — confirmed from actual .agents/skills/copywriting/ structure in this repo | read .git/config under each skill subdir for remote URL

userStories.title: Git checker — remote hash check and shallow clone
userStories.description: As a developer, I need functions to check a remote repo's HEAD hash via git ls-remote and, when the hash has changed, perform a shallow clone to discover skill folder names.
acceptanceCriteria: src/checker.ts exports checkRepo(repoUrl, cache): Promise<string[]>
checkRepo returns cached known_skills without cloning when HEAD hash is unchanged
checkRepo performs shallow clone and returns top-level folder names when hash has changed
checkRepo updates cache.repos[url] with new hash and skills list after a clone
checkRepo cleans up temp clone directory in all cases (success and failure)
checkRepo returns [] and does not throw when git ls-remote fails (silent failure)
checkRepo returns [] and does not throw when git clone fails (silent failure)
GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 is set on all git subprocess calls
src/checker.ts exports spawnCheck(client, directory): Promise<void>
spawnCheck returns immediately when config.enabled is false
spawnCheck returns immediately when elapsed time < checkIntervalMinutes
spawnCheck merges configRepos and discoveredRepos and deduplicates
spawnCheck evicts notified_skills that are no longer installed locally before comparing
spawnCheck adds newly found skills to cache.notified_skills
spawnCheck calls client.tui.showToast when new skills are found
spawnCheck does NOT call showToast when no new skills found
Unit test: checkRepo returns cached skills when hash unchanged
Unit test: checkRepo returns folder names after hash change (mocked git calls)
Unit test: checkRepo handles git ls-remote failure silently
Unit test: spawnCheck skips when disabled
Unit test: spawnCheck skips when rate-limited
Unit test: spawnCheck evicts uninstalled skills from notified_skills
/code-review passes, critical/important issues fixed
bun run typecheck passes
bun test passes
userStories.notes: src/checker.ts | uses Bun's $ shell helper for git subprocess calls | GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 env var required | shallow clone: git clone --depth 1 --quiet | ls-remote timeout: 10s, clone timeout: 30s

userStories.title: Plugin entry point
userStories.description: As an OpenCode user, I need the plugin export so OpenCode can load it as an npm plugin on session.created.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts exports a named SkillsNotifierPlugin typed as Plugin from @opencode-ai/plugin
Plugin hooks session.created and calls spawnCheck(client, directory) as fire-and-forget
session.created hook returns synchronously — it does NOT await spawnCheck
Errors from spawnCheck are swallowed and do not propagate to OpenCode
bun run build produces dist/index.js that can be required as an ES module
Unit test: session.created hook does not await (returns before spawnCheck resolves)
Unit test: errors from spawnCheck do not cause the hook to throw
/code-review passes, critical/important issues fixed
bun run typecheck passes
bun test passes
userStories.notes: src/index.ts | export SkillsNotifierPlugin | import type { Plugin } from '@opencode-ai/plugin' | spawnCheck().catch(() => {}) pattern

userStories.title: Smoke test: install and validate in OpenCode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the plugin loads correctly in a real OpenCode session and shows the toast notification when a test repo has skills not installed locally.
acceptanceCriteria: bun run build completes without errors
Plugin installed as local file plugin by symlinking or copying dist/index.js to ~/.config/opencode/plugins/opencode-skills-notifier.js
opencode.json configured with at least one test repository URL under opencode-skills-notifier.repositories
Launch opencode — no startup errors or crashes related to the plugin
Delete or rename ~/.config/opencode/skills-notifier-cache.json to force a fresh check
Relaunch opencode — toast notification appears within a few seconds listing new skills
Toast message includes skill name(s) and 'npx skills add <repo-url>' install command
Relaunch opencode again without installing skills — toast reappears (persists until installed)
Install a skill with npx skills add, then relaunch — toast no longer lists that skill
userStories.notes: Manual verification — no automated test framework can replicate a real OpenCode TUI session | also verify newline rendering in toast: if \n is not rendered, update message format in checker.ts to a single-line condensed format
